mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index reflects expanding economic output that boosts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when INDPRO is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0% constrains credit availability and slows corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when rates remain elevated.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with a falling federal funds rate signals a recessionary feedback loop where labor market deterioration overwhelms monetary easing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE increases and FEDFUNDS decreases simultaneously, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: In the aftermath of housing market contractions, an increase in housing starts signals renewed construction activity and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following months where HOUST rises from a local trough, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than half of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate below 4.0% signals a tight labor market that compresses corporate profit margins via wage pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates demand-pull expansion without immediate supply constraints, amplifying earnings optimism and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with concurrent increases in RSAFS and INDPRO see S&P 500 5-day returns negative more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten financial conditions, increasing equity risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.5% over 3 months
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.5% over a 3-month span, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates rising household income and consumption expenditure, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods of positive PAYEMS growth coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of observations.
